name: Schema Constraints as Contracts
description: Enforce a data contract via DDL constraints: PRIMARY KEY, NOT NULL, and CHECK

checks.name: fact_orders_contracted has PRIMARY KEY
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM duckdb_constraints() WHERE table_name = 'fact_orders_contracted' AND constraint_type = 'PRIMARY KEY';
checks.description: order_id must be the PRIMARY KEY

checks.name: fact_orders_contracted has at least 2 CHECK constraints
checks.query: SELECT (COUNT(*) >= 2) AS ok FROM duckdb_constraints() WHERE table_name = 'fact_orders_contracted' AND constraint_type = 'CHECK';
checks.description: At least 2 CHECK constraints: quantity > 0 and unit_price >= 0

checks.name: 5 valid rows inserted
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM fact_orders_contracted;
checks.description: Exactly 5 valid rows must be present

checks.name: No invalid quantities
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM fact_orders_contracted WHERE quantity <= 0;
checks.description: All rows must have quantity > 0 (enforced by CHECK constraint)
